name: Wombat (Northern Hairy-nosed)
conservationEfforts: Epping Forest National Park protects the main colony behind predator-proof fencing. Second population established at Richard Underwood Nature Refuge. Population grown from 35 in 1980s to ~400.
description: Australia's rarest large mammal, confined to a tiny reserve in Queensland. Northern hairy-nosed wombats build extensive underground burrow systems and are strictly nocturnal.
